description: Letter from Mūsā b. Yiṣḥaq in Sfax to Yehuda b. Moshe Ibn Sughmar in Fustat. Contains details about shipments of coins, oil and soap from the Maghreb to Fustat. The recipient is asked to buy goods, mainly flax, in Fustat. Mentions goods sent on ship of Nāṣir al-Dawla (active ca. 1062-1072) who ephemerally ruled over Alexandria and upper Egypt during the Shidda ʿUẓma in al-Mustanṣir's time. "In the ship of the Amir Nasir al-Dawla I sent you thirty-one new large skins of oil, measuring sixty-two matars; on their stoppers the names Salama and Judah are engraved. They are closed with small stoppers and fixed tightly with soaked palm leaves and parchment. With them are two leather bags with soap, weighing five qintars. The names Judah and Salama are engraved on these as well. I hope everything will arrive. It is all in the hands of 'Abd al-Rahman b. All b. Habib Sfaqsi." (Goitein, LMJT, pg. 139-140).
tags: ibn sughmar, trade, Nasir al-Dawla, flax, coin
description_he: מכתב ממוסא בן יצחק בספקס ליהודה בן משה אבן סוגמאר בפוסטאט. פרטים על משלוחי מטבעות, שמן וסבון מהמגרב לפוסטאט. הנמען מתבקש לקנות סחורות, בעיקר פשתן. 'בספינת האמיר נאצר אל-דולה שלחתי אליך 31 נאד שמן חדשים גדולים... עליהם חקוקים שמות סלאמה ויהודה.'
